questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the Khilafat Movement's activities in Lucknow, what was the primary objective of the movement as per the historical records of the period?

options.text: To demand the immediate withdrawal of the British from the Indian subcontinent.
To protect the authority of the Ottoman Empire and create a Caliphate.
To negotiate for separate electorates for the Muslim community in the United Provinces.
To establish a secular democratic government in Turkey.

questions.explanation: The Khilafat Movement was primarily concerned with the religious and political status of the Sultan of Turkey, who was the Caliph. Its objective was to protect the Ottoman Empire's authority and create a Caliphate, which in turn served as a platform for Hindu-Muslim unity against British rule in India.
questions.takeaway: The Khilafat Movement's core objective was the protection of the Ottoman Caliphate, using it as a rallying point for anti-colonial unity.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Objective: The movement aimed to create a Caliphate and protect the Ottoman Empire's authority. Unity: It brought together Hindu and Muslim leaders in a common cause against colonial rule.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Ambica Charan Majumdar, the President of the 1916 Congress session, famously stated, 'If the Congress was buried at Surat, it is reborn in Lucknow.' Which of the following best captures the essence of this 'rebirth'?

options.text: The transition of the Congress from an elite-led organization to a mass-based revolutionary party.
The formal reconciliation of the Moderates and Extremists and the cooperation with the Muslim League.
The shifting of the Congress headquarters from Calcutta to the garden of Wajid Ali Shah in Lucknow.
The adoption of the Non-Cooperation resolution for the first time in an official session.

questions.explanation: The 'burial' at Surat refers to the 1907 split between Moderates and Extremists. The 'rebirth' at Lucknow in 1916 refers to their reunification (facilitated by Annie Besant) and the signing of the Lucknow Pact, which brought the Congress and the Muslim League together on a common platform.
questions.takeaway: The 1916 Lucknow Session is characterized by the 'reunion' of Congress factions and the 'union' with the Muslim League, reversing the damage of the 1907 Surat split.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The Lucknow Pact was signed and moderates and extremists came together through the efforts of Annie Besant during this session. 'If the Congress was buried at Surat, it is reborn in Lucknow in the garden of Wajid Ali Shah.' — Ambica Charan Majumdar, Congress President
